tagline: Calculate extra fabric needed to account for shrinkage
keywords: fabric shrinkage, pre-wash, sewing, fabric allowance
calcFn: var cutLength = length / (1 - shrinkPct/100); var cutWidth = width / (1 - shrinkPct/100); var extraFabric = (cutLength * cutWidth) - (length * width); return {cutLength: fmt(cutLength,1), cutWidth: fmt(cutWidth,1), extraFabric: fmt(extraFabric,0)};

faq.a: Cotton typically shrinks 3-5% on first wash. Some fabrics shrink up to 10%.
Yes, always pre-wash to preshrink before cutting, especially for garments.

formulas.name: Cut Size
formulas.explanation: Divide desired size by shrinkage factor.

tagline: Calculate binding strip length for your quilt
keywords: quilt binding, binding yardage, quilting, bias binding
calcFn: var perimeter = 2 * (length + width) + 12; var strips = Math.ceil(perimeter / 42); var fabricInches = strips * bindingWidth; var yardage = fabricInches / 36; return {perimeter: fmt(perimeter,0), strips: fmt(strips,0), yardage: fmt(yardage,2)};

faq.a: 2.5 inches is standard for double-fold binding, giving a 3/8 inch finished edge.

faq.q: How much extra should I add?
faq.a: Add 10-12 inches to the perimeter for joining strips and turning corners.

formulas.name: Binding Length
formulas.explanation: Perimeter plus extra for corners and joins.

tagline: Calculate elastic needed for waistbands and cuffs
calcFn: var ratios = {'Regular Knit': 0.85, 'Woven Non-Roll': 0.90, 'Fold-Over': 0.80, 'Clear Elastic': 0.75}; var ratio = ratios[elasticType] || 0.85; var cutLength = (bodyMeasure * ratio) + overlap; var stretchRatio = ((1/ratio) - 1) * 100; return {cutLength: fmt(cutLength,1), stretchRatio: fmt(stretchRatio,0)};

faq.q: How much shorter should elastic be?
faq.a: Cut elastic 10-25% shorter than the body measurement depending on type.
Yes, wider elastic distributes pressure better and rolls less.

formulas.name: Elastic Length
formulas.explanation: Body measurement reduced by stretch ratio plus joining overlap.

tagline: Calculate total fabric needed including seam allowances
keywords: seam allowance, pattern cutting, sewing, extra fabric
calcFn: var cutLength = patternLength + (seamAllowance * 2); var cutWidth = patternWidth + (seamAllowance * 2); var totalFabric = cutLength * cutWidth * pieces; return {cutLength: fmt(cutLength,2), cutWidth: fmt(cutWidth,2), totalFabric: fmt(totalFabric,0)};

faq.a: 5/8 inch (1.5cm) for garments, 1/4 inch for quilting.

faq.q: When to use wider seam allowance?
faq.a: Use 1 inch for loosely woven fabrics or areas that need adjustment room.

formulas.name: Cut Size
formulas.explanation: Add seam allowance to both sides of each edge.

tagline: Determine the right zipper length for your project
keywords: zipper, zipper length, sewing zipper, zipper size
calcFn: var addInches = {'Dress (center back)': 1, 'Skirt (side)': 0.5, 'Pants (fly)': 0.5, 'Jacket (separating)': 0, 'Bag/Pouch': -1, 'Pillow': -2}; var types = {'Dress (center back)': 'Invisible zipper', 'Skirt (side)': 'Invisible zipper', 'Pants (fly)': 'Metal jean zipper', 'Jacket (separating)': 'Separating zipper', 'Bag/Pouch': 'Nylon coil', 'Pillow': 'Nylon coil'}; var zipperLength = opening + (addInches[application] || 0); var type = types[application] || 'Standard'; return {zipperLength: fmt(zipperLength,1), type: type};

faq.q: How do I choose zipper length?
faq.a: Match the opening length. For dresses, add 1 inch for the hook and eye above.
Yes, nylon and polyester zippers can be shortened by sewing a new stop.

formulas.name: Zipper Length
formulas.explanation: Opening length plus or minus based on garment type.

tagline: Calculate interfacing needed for garment construction
calcFn: var totalArea = pieces * avgLength * avgWidth * 1.1; var rows = Math.ceil(pieces * avgWidth / interfacingWidth); var linearInches = rows * avgLength; var yardage = Math.ceil(linearInches / 36 * 10) / 10; return {totalArea: fmt(totalArea,0), yardage: fmt(yardage,1)};

faq.a: A stabilizer fused or sewn to fabric to add structure to collars, cuffs, and waistbands.
Fusible is easier and works for most fabrics. Sew-in is better for delicates and textured fabrics.

formulas.name: Yardage
formulas.explanation: Total linear interfacing based on layout.

tagline: Calculate depth of field from aperture, focal length, and distance
keywords: depth of field, DOF, aperture, focal length, photography
calcFn: var coc = {'Full Frame': 0.03, 'APS-C': 0.02, 'Micro 4/3': 0.015}; var c = coc[sensor] || 0.03; var distMM = distance * 304.8; var h = (focal * focal) / (aperture * c); var nearLimit = (distMM * (h - focal)) / (h + distMM - 2*focal); var farLimit = (distMM * (h - focal)) / (h - distMM); if (farLimit < 0) farLimit = Infinity; var dof = farLimit === Infinity ? 'Infinite' : fmt((farLimit - nearLimit)/304.8, 2); nearLimit = nearLimit / 304.8; farLimit = farLimit === Infinity ? 'Infinity' : fmt(farLimit/304.8, 1); return {dof: dof, nearLimit: fmt(nearLimit,1), farLimit: farLimit};

faq.a: Aperture (wider=shallower), focal length (longer=shallower), and distance (closer=shallower).
The largest blur spot that still appears sharp. Varies by sensor size.

formulas.name: Hyperfocal Distance
formulas.explanation: Distance beyond which everything is in focus.

tagline: Estimate golden hour timing for your location
keywords: golden hour, photography lighting, sunset, sunrise, magic hour
calcFn: var monthIdx = ['January','February','March','April','May','June','July','August','September','October','November','December'].indexOf(month); var absLat = Math.abs(latitude); var baseDuration = 30; var latBonus = absLat * 0.3; var seasonMod = Math.abs(monthIdx - 5.5) <= 3 ? 5 : -5; if (absLat > 50) seasonMod *= 2; var duration = baseDuration + latBonus + seasonMod; var quality = absLat > 35 ? 'Rich warm tones' : 'Bright golden light'; return {duration: fmt(Math.max(duration, 15),0), quality: quality};

faq.a: The hour after sunrise and before sunset when light is warm and soft.

faq.q: Why is golden hour better for photos?
faq.a: Low sun angle creates warm colors, long shadows, and diffused light.

formulas.name: Duration
formulas.explanation: Higher latitudes have longer golden hours, especially in winter.

tagline: Calculate required megapixels for your print size
keywords: print resolution, megapixels, DPI, photo print, large print
calcFn: var pixelWidth = printWidth * dpi; var pixelHeight = printHeight * dpi; var megapixels = (pixelWidth * pixelHeight) / 1000000; return {megapixels: fmt(megapixels,1), pixelWidth: fmt(pixelWidth,0), pixelHeight: fmt(pixelHeight,0)};

faq.a: 300 DPI for close viewing, 150 DPI for posters, 72 DPI for billboards.
AI upscaling can help, but starting with enough megapixels is always better.

formulas.name: Megapixels
formulas.explanation: Total pixels needed divided by one million.

tagline: Calculate flash exposure using guide number
keywords: flash guide number, flash photography, exposure, GN
calcFn: var isoFactor = Math.sqrt(iso / 100); var effectiveGN = guideNumber * isoFactor; var aperture = effectiveGN / distance; var maxDistance = effectiveGN / 4; return {aperture: fmt(aperture,1), maxDistance: fmt(maxDistance,1)};

faq.a: A rating of flash power. Higher GN means more powerful flash. GN = distance × f-stop.
Higher ISO effectively increases the guide number, allowing greater distance or smaller aperture.

formulas.name: Guide Number
formulas.explanation: Relationship between flash power, distance, and aperture.

tagline: Calculate interval settings for time-lapse photography
keywords: time lapse, interval, timelapse, photography, video
calcFn: var totalFrames = clipLength * fps; var eventSeconds = eventDuration * 60; var interval = eventSeconds / totalFrames; var storageGB = totalFrames * 25 / 1024; return {totalFrames: fmt(totalFrames,0), interval: fmt(interval,1), storageGB: fmt(storageGB,1)};

faq.a: 1-2s for fast action, 5-10s for clouds, 15-30s for construction/plants.

faq.q: How many photos do I need?
faq.a: Multiply desired video length by frame rate (e.g., 30 seconds × 30 fps = 900 frames).

formulas.name: Interval
formulas.explanation: Total recording time divided by frames needed.

tagline: Estimate photo file sizes for storage planning
keywords: photo file size, RAW, JPEG, storage, megapixels
calcFn: var sizeFactors = {'JPEG Fine': 0.35, 'JPEG Normal': 0.2, 'RAW': 1.5, 'RAW + JPEG': 1.85}; var factor = sizeFactors[format] || 0.35; var perPhoto = megapixels * factor; var totalGB = (perPhoto * photos) / 1024; var cards = Math.ceil(totalGB / 30); return {perPhoto: fmt(perPhoto,1), totalGB: fmt(totalGB,1), cards: fmt(cards,0)};

faq.q: How big is a RAW file?
faq.a: Typically 1.5-2× the megapixel count in MB (e.g., 24MP = 36-48MB RAW).
RAW retains all sensor data for editing. JPEG is compressed and smaller.

formulas.name: File Size
formulas.explanation: Megapixels multiplied by compression factor per format.

tagline: Calculate monthly feeding costs for your dog
keywords: dog food cost, feeding cost, dog budget, pet food
calcFn: var baseCups = weight * 0.04 + 0.5; var actMult = {'Low': 0.8, 'Moderate': 1, 'High': 1.3, 'Working Dog': 1.6}; var cupsPerDay = baseCups * (actMult[activity] || 1); var lbsPerMonth = cupsPerDay * 0.25 * 30; var prices = {'Budget ($1/lb)': 1, 'Mid-Range ($2/lb)': 2, 'Premium ($3.50/lb)': 3.5, 'Raw/Fresh ($5/lb)': 5}; var monthlyCost = lbsPerMonth * (prices[foodQuality] || 2); return {cupsPerDay: fmt(cupsPerDay,1), lbsPerMonth: fmt(lbsPerMonth,1), monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost)};

faq.q: How much should I feed my dog?
faq.a: Generally 1/2 cup per 10-15 lbs of body weight per day, adjusted for activity.

faq.q: Is premium dog food worth it?
faq.a: Higher-quality food often means better nutrition, fewer health issues, and smaller portions.

formulas.name: Daily Cups
formulas.explanation: Base feeding amount scaled by dog size and activity.

tagline: Calculate how many fish your tank can support
keywords: aquarium stocking, fish tank, gallons per fish, fish capacity
calcFn: var inchesPerGal = {'Standard': 1, 'Over-filtered (2x)': 1.3, 'Under-filtered': 0.7}; var avgSize = {'Small (1-2 inches)': 1.5, 'Medium (2-4 inches)': 3, 'Large (4-6 inches)': 5, 'Extra Large (6+ inches)': 8}; var totalInches = gallons * (inchesPerGal[filtration] || 1); var fishInches = avgSize[fishSize] || 3; var maxFish = Math.floor(totalInches / fishInches); var recommendedFish = Math.floor(maxFish * 0.75); return {maxFish: fmt(maxFish,0), recommendedFish: fmt(recommendedFish,0)};

faq.a: One inch of fish per gallon of water. It's a guideline, not exact.

faq.q: Can I overstock with good filtration?
faq.a: Over-filtration allows slightly more fish, but never exceed 130% of the guideline.

formulas.name: Fish Capacity
formulas.formula: Fish = (Gallons × Filtration Factor) / Avg Fish Size
formulas.explanation: Total inches of fish the tank can support.

tagline: Calculate monthly cat litter consumption and cost
keywords: cat litter, litter usage, cat care, litter cost
calcFn: var basePerCat = {'Clumping Clay': 7, 'Non-Clumping Clay': 10, 'Crystal/Silica': 5, 'Pine/Natural': 8, 'Corn/Wheat': 7}; var lbsPerWeek = (basePerCat[litterType] || 7) * cats; var monthlyLbs = lbsPerWeek * 4.33; var bagsPerMonth = Math.ceil(monthlyLbs / bagLbs); var monthlyCost = bagsPerMonth * pricePerBag; return {monthlyLbs: fmt(monthlyLbs,0), bagsPerMonth: fmt(bagsPerMonth,0), monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost)};

faq.a: About 7-10 lbs per week per cat depending on litter type.

faq.q: How often should I change litter?
faq.a: Scoop daily, full change every 1-2 weeks for clumping, weekly for non-clumping.

formulas.name: Monthly Litter
formulas.explanation: Weekly usage times number of cats times weeks per month.

tagline: Calculate weight-based medication dosing for pets
keywords: pet medication, dosage, veterinary, pet health, weight dosing
calcFn: var dosePerAdmin = weight * doseRate; var tabletsPerDose = dosePerAdmin / tabletMg; var dailyDose = dosePerAdmin * frequency; return {dosePerAdmin: fmt(dosePerAdmin,1), tabletsPerDose: fmt(tabletsPerDose,1), dailyDose: fmt(dailyDose,1)};

faq.q: Can I use human medication for pets?
faq.a: Some human medications are safe for pets at proper doses, but always consult your vet first.

faq.q: What if the dose is between tablet sizes?
faq.a: Ask your vet about splitting tablets or using liquid formulations.

tagline: Calculate calories burned walking your dog
keywords: dog walking, calories, exercise, walking calories
calcFn: var mets = {'Slow (2 mph)': 2.5, 'Moderate (3 mph)': 3.5, 'Brisk (4 mph)': 4.5, 'Jogging (5 mph)': 7}; var met = mets[pace] || 3.5; var calories = met * 3.5 * (yourWeight * 0.453592) / 200 * minutes; var weeklyCalories = calories * 7; var speeds = {'Slow (2 mph)': 2, 'Moderate (3 mph)': 3, 'Brisk (4 mph)': 4, 'Jogging (5 mph)': 5}; var monthlyMiles = (speeds[pace] || 3) * (minutes/60) * 30; return {calories: fmt(calories,0), weeklyCalories: fmt(weeklyCalories,0), monthlyMiles: fmt(monthlyMiles,0)};

faq.q: How many calories does dog walking burn?
faq.a: About 120-200 calories per 30 minutes depending on pace and your weight.

faq.q: How long should I walk my dog?
faq.a: Most dogs need 30-60 minutes of walking per day.

formulas.name: Calories
formulas.explanation: MET-based calorie calculation from body weight and duration.

tagline: Calculate if pet insurance is worth it for your pet
keywords: pet insurance, pet health cost, insurance value, vet bills
calcFn: var remainingYears = Math.max(12 - age, 1); var lifetimePremium = monthlyPremium * 12 * remainingYears; var avgAnnual = {'Low (mixed/hardy breed)': 800, 'Medium (average breed)': 1200, 'High (prone to issues)': 2000}; var expectedVet = (avgAnnual[breed] || 1200) * remainingYears; var verdict = expectedVet > lifetimePremium ? 'Likely worth it - expected vet costs exceed premiums' : 'May not be worth it - consider a vet savings fund instead'; return {lifetimePremium: dollar(lifetimePremium), expectedVet: dollar(expectedVet), verdict: verdict};

faq.a: For breeds prone to health issues, insurance often pays for itself. For healthy breeds, a savings account may be better.

faq.q: When should I get pet insurance?
faq.a: As young as possible — pre-existing conditions are never covered.

formulas.name: Lifetime Cost
formulas.explanation: Total premiums over the pet's expected remaining life.
